articles.title: Japan’s Takaichi to Revamp Indo-Pacific Vision in Vietnam Speech
articles.description: Japanese Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi on Saturday will use a speech in Vietnam to lay out her vision for the Indo-Pacific, as she seeks to bolster economic and security ties across the region in a move that could counter an increasingly assertive China.

articles.title: Price of commercial LPG, small cylinders hiked
articles.description: Prices remain the same for domestic LPG and aviation turbine fuel for scheduled domestic airlines

articles.title: Abu Dhabi’s OPEC exit begins its ascent of ‘peak oil’
articles.description: The UAE’s exit marks a potential turning point for global oil governance

articles.title: ​Primary league: On the Congress, UDF, 2026 Kerala Assembly elections
articles.description: In Kerala, the UDF is engaged in ugly manoeuvres for the CM’s post

articles.title: Tamil Nadu to experience hotter May, with above-normal rainfall
articles.description: In its outlook, the IMD has also outlined the likely impact of increased temperature, including on agriculture, and issued an agromet advisory

articles.title: Karnataka launches India’s first state-led spacetech CoE in Bengaluru
articles.description: Karnataka has launched India's first state-led Centre of Excellence for Space Technology (CoE SpaceTech Foundation) in Bengaluru. This initiative aims to bridge the gap between research and market-ready solutions by fostering applied innovation, talent development, and ecosystem building. The Centre will serve as a collaborative platform for government, industry, startups, and academia to drive India's space economy.

articles.title: Meta faces US lawmaker scrutiny over removal of lawyer ads for social media addiction cases
articles.description: Two US senators have written to Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg. They criticize the company's decision to remove advertisements. These ads were from attorneys seeking clients harmed by social media platforms. The attorneys aimed to recruit plaintiffs for lawsuits. These lawsuits accuse companies like Meta of fueling a youth mental health crisis. Senators state this removal preserves a harmful business model.

articles.title: Letters to The Editor — May 2, 2026
‘Election bill’, says Rahul Gandhi on commercial LPG price hike; Kharge slams ‘anti-labour’ government
articles.description: Rahul Gandhi says the latest increase is proof that burden of inflation would intensify after polls; K.C. Venugopal says hike will affect ‘crores’; Kharge slams Modi govt. on International Labour Day

articles.title: Government to sustain capex push despite fiscal stress due to global uncertainties: Finance Ministry official
articles.description: He said highways, railways, shipping, ports, and urban development sectors would be the focus areas for FY27 capex

articles.title: Two-wheeler EV sales drop in April after March peak; Ola Electric's market share improves
articles.description: India's electric two-wheeler market experienced a significant 22% dip in April registrations, falling to 1.48 lakh units after a March peak. Despite a broad decline, Ola Electric saw a notable 20% surge in sales, improving its market share. This slowdown follows a strong FY26 growth driven by legacy automakers and aggressive March sales tactics.

articles.title: Eating out, international travel costlier after commercial LPG, ATF price hike
articles.description: International travel, too, might get more expensive, with prices of aviation turbine fuel (ATF) for international flights being raised, according to a statement by state-run refiner Indian Oil Corporation.

articles.title: Science explains why aerobic exercise works best for knee osteoarthritis relief
RBI says 98.47% of ₹2000 banknotes returned
articles.description: The total value of ₹2000 banknotes in circulation has declined from ₹3.56 lakh crore on May 19, 2023 to ₹5,451 crore

articles.title: India-New Zealand FTA: Shielding trade from great power rivalry
Who ‘surrendered’ to the US? That’s the wrong question
The fear factor: Why West Bengal’s voter turnout shattered records
Mustard oil or olive oil: Gurugram doctor settles debate – ‘Stop outsourcing decisions’
articles.description: In Indian cooking, mustard and peanut oils are preferred for high-heat methods, according to Dr Kaushal. He also warns against using olive oil at high temperatures and advocates for oil rotation to improve health outcomes.

articles.title: Interview | Author Sian Hughes on exploring the idea of the invisible middle-aged woman
articles.description: An abrasive heroine navigating trauma and survival is at the centre of Hughes’ new novel No Such Thing as Monday

articles.title: Artemis timeline shifts: Can NASA still land astronauts on the Moon in 2028?
Big Tech Q1 2026 earnings: Key takeaways on AI spending, cloud growth, and more
Pentagon reaches agreements with leading AI companies
articles.description: The Pentagon has struck deals with seven major AI firms. These companies will integrate their advanced AI into the Defense Department's secure networks. This move aims to boost data analysis and improve decision-making for soldiers. It signals a shift towards an AI-first military. Meanwhile, Anthropic faces restrictions due to security concerns.

articles.title: Airport of the future? Tokyo tests humanoid robots for ground operations
In a system where even dignity requires documentation, who’s the real villain of Jitu Munda’s story?
PhonePe’s Share.Market CEO Ujjwal Jain steps down
articles.description: Ujjwal Jain, CEO of PhonePe's stock trading platform Share.Market, has departed after four years, calling it a "relaunch" for his next venture. Jain, who founded WealthDesk and OpenQ before their acquisition by PhonePe, led the launch of Share.Market in August 2023. This move occurs as PhonePe has postponed its IPO plans.

articles.title: The Devil doesn’t just wear Prada. She locates our politics in it
SpaceX spending on Starship tops $15 billion in rush for airline-like rocketry
articles.description: SpaceX has poured over $15 billion into its Starship rocket. This next-generation system is key for launching more Starlink satellites and future moon and Mars missions. The company aims for rapid reusability. Starship's success is vital for SpaceX's ambitious plans and its public market debut.

articles.title: GST revenue follows annual pattern, hits all-time high of Rs 2.43 lakh crore in April 2026
articles.description: Growth was once again driven by collections on imports, with revenue from domestic sales growing slower.

articles.title: IPO-bound JSW One acquires proptech platform BuildNext, expands home-construction wares
articles.description: IPO-bound JSW One Platforms has acquired BuildNext Construction Solutions, a proptech startup backed by Pidilite Ventures, to enhance its integrated home construction and renovation offerings. This move aims to address inefficiencies in India's fragmented housing market by combining BuildNext's design and execution expertise with JSW One's supply chain strengths, promising a more organized and predictable home-building experience for customers.

articles.title: India, U.K. discuss ways to boost bilateral trade
articles.description: The bilateral trade was discussed during a virtual meeting between Commerce and Industry Minister Piyush Goyal and U.K. Secretary of State for Business and Trade Peter Kyle

articles.title: India faces cybersecurity talent crunch as AI, cloud drive demand: Report
Hyderabad cops want RBI to plug gaps that facilitate cybercrime
articles.description: Hyderabad Police's crackdown on cyber fraud, revealing systemic banking gaps and Rs 400 crore annual losses, has prompted a high-level meeting. Discussions focused on coordinated action against mule accounts and faster response systems, with banks urged to adopt RBI's MuleHunter.AI tool to combat digital financial crimes.

articles.title: Energy trade-offs: What’s prompted the global shift towards renewables over fossil fuels?
articles.description: As renewables reach record highs, the war in Iran has triggered a fresh energy shock with complex policy implications. While past crises accelerated clean energy adoption, today’s inflation and supply disruptions could slow it down. Which forces will prevail?

articles.title: An AI bubble or a psychological domino? Why investors mustn’t confuse OpenAI’s health with that of the whole AI industry
articles.description: Market activity this week has shown that investors are conflating OpenAI with the wider artificial intelligence industry. But is that sound judgment? A closer look would show that the industry has evolved since the initial days when OpenAI was the biggest fish in the pond.

articles.title: For, Gen Z — the most affected by changing nature of work — it’s time to reclaim labour politics
Drone maker ideaForge soars to profit in Q4; FY26 revenue surges 40% to Rs 226 crore
articles.description: Drone maker ideaForge Technology reported a significant turnaround in FY26, with Q4 revenue soaring to Rs 141 crore and achieving a profit of Rs 60 crore. The company secured its highest-ever annual bookings and saw early international traction, including its first US order, while expanding into combat drone capabilities.

articles.title: Google Gemini adds file generation feature for documents and spreadsheets
Tim Cook says he is ‘over the moon’ as Apple eyes massive growth in India
What it takes to move heat action plans from advisories to mandates
articles.description: States and think-tanks are innovating to fund, implement, and track climate and heat action plans

articles.title: Centre, states collect record  ₹2.43 trillion in gross April GST revenue
articles.description: Last April, the Centre and states had collected ₹2.37 trillion in GST revenue before adjusting for refunds, but the figures are not strictly comparable because of the GST cuts in September 2025.

articles.title: UPI hits record scale: 24,162 crore transactions worth ₹314 lakh crore in FY26
articles.description: The government reiterated its commitment to strengthening the digital payments ecosystem, with a focus on innovation, security, and inclusion, as UPI continues to scale new milestones

articles.title: UPI volumes report 3% dip in April to 22.5 billion transactions after record high numbers in March
articles.description: UPI transactions in April saw a minor dip from March but a 25% rise year-on-year. While UPI shows resilience, India's digital payments sector is experiencing a slowdown, with new user growth also decelerating. NPCI is considering market share caps for larger UPI apps.

articles.title: City Nature Challenge 2026: Andhra, Telangana record surge
articles.description: A four-day global citizen science effort where cities document urban biodiversity, building valuable data through public participation

articles.title: On May Day, a workforce in India without a floor
articles.description: From Noida’s streets to furnace rooms in Chhattisgarh, India’s new labour regime delivers for employers — and for workers, what it long warned of
